sections.opening: Come, the candle is lit and the cards await. After you've passed through all the stormy energies of the Wheel, the time has come for a card that brings order to chaos — Justice. This is the card that reminds us the universe operates according to laws, that there's a system of perfect balance of which you are part. When I see Justice in a reading, I know — the time for truth has come. Not someone else's truth, but the deep cosmic truth that has been guiding us for thousands of years. The woman sitting on the throne, holding scales of justice in one hand and a sword in the other — she's not here to judge you, she's here to help you find the balance within yourself. The number 11 vibrates deeply within this card, reminding us that duality and justice go hand in hand.
sections.theoryA:
What is the meaning of the Justice card in Tarot?

Justice in Tarot represents divine balance and cosmic truth. The card reminds us that there are higher laws governing the world, and every action leads to an appropriate reaction. The figure on the card sits between pillars of wisdom, holding scales as a symbol of balance and fairness, and a sword as a symbol of cutting through to truth and separating right from wrong.

In Tarot tradition, Justice represents the cosmic principle of karma — what a person sows, they shall reap. This isn't punishment or reward, but a basic natural law that always operates in the background. When the card appears in a reading, it invites you to look at your situation through the lens of fairness and truth.

In upright position, Justice indicates the arrival of truth clarification, making informed decisions based on facts, and recognizing the need to take responsibility for actions. This is the time when truth rises to the surface, and there's an opportunity to correct wrongs.

But Justice isn't only about external judgment — it's primarily about inner balance. The card reminds you to find the point where all parts of your personality are in harmony — heart and mind, intuition and logic, emotion and thought. It's a delicate balance that requires deep listening to all the voices within you.

practice1.instruction: Take the Justice card from your deck and examine it deeply. Focus on the scales in her hand — let your eyes rest on the pans. Ask yourself: In which area of your life do you currently feel imbalance? Write what you see.

sections.theoryB:
How is the Justice card interpreted in reversed position?

In reversed position, Justice warns of imbalance and distorted judgment. This doesn't mean truth disappears, but rather that you might not be seeing it clearly right now. You might be judging yourself too harshly, or conversely — ignoring responsibility you need to take.

The reversed card can hint at unfair trials, misguided decisions, or situations where justice isn't done. Sometimes this manifests as excessive self-punishment or avoiding making difficult decisions that need to be made. Your inner judge might be too harsh or too lenient.

Spiritually, the reversed card reminds us that true justice sometimes appears slow to human eyes, but it always arrives. When you see injustices around you and suffer from it, reversed Justice tells you: let time work. Sometimes the most just path is not to interfere.

The astrological connection is important here — Justice is linked to Libra, the air sign that always seeks the perfect point of balance. When the card is reversed, it's like scales that aren't properly calibrated — all their readings will be wrong until you fix the basic problem.

practice2.instruction: Think of a situation in your life where you feel justice wasn't done. Write a short letter to that situation, but as the cosmic judge — what would you say to all parties involved? How would you bring balance to the situation? Write from the perspective of a wise soul.

sections.deepDive:
What's not written in books about Justice

Here's a secret most courses don't tell: Justice isn't a judge, she's a mirror. Her scales aren't interested in measuring who's right and who's wrong — they want to show you where your truth stands right now. Every time this card appears, it's essentially saying: stop looking for justice outside and start finding it within yourself.

In Kabbalah, Justice is connected to the sephirah of Yesod — the sephirah representing solid foundations and moral systems. But Yesod isn't rigidity, it's a foundation that sways and adjusts constantly according to need. Like a tree that bends in the wind but doesn't break.

The great secret of Justice is that it doesn't work on linear time as we're accustomed to think. What appears to you as injustice today, might reveal itself as growth tomorrow. The game is much longer than what we see from our small angle. Justice sees the complete picture — that's why she can be so calm.

questions.question: What do the scales in Justice's hand represent?
questions.explanation: The scales represent balance and fairness — the principle guiding the card is finding the wise and just point, not rigid judgment

questions.question: Which number marks the Justice card in the Major Arcana system?
questions.explanation: Justice is marked with number 11, representing balance, duality and harmony between opposing forces

questions.question: Which astrological sign is Justice connected to?
questions.explanation: Justice is connected to Libra, the sign that seeks balance, harmony and fairness in relationships

questions.question: What does the sword in Justice's other hand symbolize?
options: Cutting through truth and separating right from wrong
questions.explanation: The sword symbolizes her ability to cut through illusions and separate truth from falsehood, just like a sword of truth

questions.question: When Justice appears in reversed position, what does it warn about?
questions.explanation: Reversed Justice warns of imbalance, unfair judgment, or situations where one doesn't see the situation clearly

closing.summary: Justice reminds you that the universe operates according to laws higher than our immediate understanding. Every situation in your life is an opportunity to find deeper balance — between heart and mind, between giving and receiving, between action and rest. Truth isn't always comfortable, but it's always healing.
closing.wisdom: True justice begins from within and radiates outward.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we'll meet the Hanged Man — the card that teaches us how to see from a completely new angle...

faq.question: Is Justice related to courts and legal matters?
faq.answer: Yes and no. Justice can indicate lawsuits or legal decisions, but it primarily speaks about cosmic justice and spiritual balance. Sometimes the legal meaning is relevant, but it's always worth checking what the broader context is.

faq.question: What's the difference between Justice and Strength in Tarot?
faq.answer: Strength speaks about inner and spiritual control, while Justice focuses on balance and fairness. Strength is more personal, Justice is more universal — it relates to laws that guide the entire universe.

faq.question: How do I know when Justice speaks about inner balance versus external justice?
faq.answer: Listen to the surrounding cards and the question you posed. If the question was about relationships or external situation — it's likely about external justice. If the question was about feelings or personal growth — it's probably about inner balance.

faq.question: Why does Justice sometimes feel cold and distant?
faq.answer: Because Justice sees the complete picture, not just the present moment. What seems harsh to you now, might reveal itself as wisdom tomorrow. Justice operates on cosmic time, not human time.

seo.title: Justice in Tarot - Card Meaning, Balance and Truth | Maktuba Academy
seo.description: Learn about the Justice card in Tarot - number 11 in the Major Arcana. Meanings in upright and reversed positions, astrological connections and practical exercises with the cosmic priestess.
